title: Something every heart is loving
textAttribution: Text: Gerhardt Tersteegen (1697–1769), Emma Frances Shuttleworth Bevan (1827–1909).
publicDomainBasis: Gerhardt Tersteegen (1697–1769), Emma Frances Shuttleworth Bevan (1827–1909), John Stainer (1840–1901). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
Verse 2
Verse 3
sections.text:
Bright with all eternal radiance
Is the glory of Thy face;
Thou art loving, sweet, and tender,
Full of pity, full of grace.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
When I hated, Thou didst love me,
Shedd’st for me Thy precious blood;
Still Thou lovest, lovest ever,
Shall I not love Thee, my God?

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Keep my heart still faithful to Thee,
That my earthly life may be
But a shadow to that glory
Of my hidden life in Thee.
